methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

private.name: Credo Semiconductor

data.narrative:
Ouster operates in Chips & Semiconductors as a public company currently valued at $1.5B, having raised over $500M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 12 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables spanning both private and public stages, including companies like PsiQuantum ($7.0B), Etched ($5.0B), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), and Tenstorrent ($2.7B).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given available data. The 12-company peer set yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $500M at the low end to $7.0B at the high end. Public-comp anchoring using semiconductor sector multiples (median P/S of 22.3x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) is not applicable here, as target revenue figures are not on file—a common constraint in private company valuation work.

The peer-median method supports a range of $1.8B to $2.5B (median $2.2B). Ouster's current $1.5B valuation sits below this implied range, though the wide spread of peer valuations ($500M–$7.0B) reflects material heterogeneity within frontier-chip subsectors. Key uncertainties include near-term revenue trajectory, competitive positioning relative to the peer cohort, and technology risk—dimensions the median statistic does not capture.
